response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with genuine geospatial interest (GISCollective, Green Map) and distributed systems exposure (Dunnhumby micro-services at scale), but his core technical profile is fundamentally misaligned with the role. The position requires active, modern C++ or Rust expertise as its primary technical bar; Bogdan's C++ use is limited to a 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis sound server, with no commercial C++ work since. His current and recent work is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js. There is no Rust experience, no routing or navigation pipeline work, and the geospatial experience, while present, is at the application/platform layer rather than the low-level tiling and data-pipeline layer the role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map — genuine familiarity with geo-data concepts and open-source GIS tooling.
Demonstrated distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O patterns.
Strong breadth across the stack and a track record of picking up new problem domains, including embedded systems and hardware tooling.
concerns: No active or modern C++ or Rust experience — the last C/C++ work was 2014–2015 and is not commercial-grade navigation or tiling work.
Geospatial work is application/platform-level (data collection, visualization) rather than routing-tile pipelines, tiling algorithms, or navigation guidance systems.
Primary skill set (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React) is entirely outside the required technology stack with no signal of transition toward C++ or systems programming.
